activity: UMC 22/28nm and specialty wafer-fab expansion and ramp execution.
investment_amount: UMC announced US$5 billion Singapore 22/28nm fab expansion.
capacity_workforce_indicator: Project-specific P3 ramp, customer qualification, and utilization milestones are internal data gaps.
why_it_matters_for_umc: This is the controlled execution path already tied to UMC customers, operations, and capital planning.
what_it_supports: Primary focus on Singapore Fab 12i P3 execution before new external expansion.
what_it_does_not_prove: It does not prove that an additional non-Singapore investment is justified.
next_validation_step: Track P3 ramp by customer/product family, utilization, margin, and capex discipline.
decision_implication: Baseline anchor case for comparing any post-P3 option.

activity: Packaging, assembly, test, equipment, materials, and semiconductor engineering ecosystem.
scale_indicator: Penang has over 350 foreign firms and 4,000 SMEs; Malaysia accounts for about 13% of global back-end semiconductor activity.
investment_amount: Cluster-level figure varies by project; data gap for UMC-specific partner capex.
capacity_workforce_indicator: Representative companies include Intel, Infineon, AMD, Micron, ASE, AT&S, Inari Amertron, and Unisem.
why_it_matters_for_umc: The cluster could support packaging / OSAT partnership diligence adjacent to Fab 12i output.
what_it_supports: Malaysia as conditional Packaging / OSAT watchlist.
what_it_does_not_prove: It does not prove UMC customer demand, packaging attach-rate, partner economics, incentives, or ROIC.
next_validation_step: Validate customer overlap, partner shortlist, attach-rate, revenue model, incentives, and ROIC.
decision_implication: Supports considering a partner-first packaging / OSAT workstream, subject to UMC internal validation.

scale_indicator: Large multinational packaging and assembly/test footprint in Malaysia.
investment_amount: Intel announced a major Malaysia expansion; source-specific amount should be confirmed before investment committee use.
capacity_workforce_indicator: Workforce and package-capacity details require source-level refresh.
why_it_matters_for_umc: Shows Malaysia's ability to host sophisticated back-end semiconductor operations.
what_it_does_not_prove: Intel's internal packaging economics do not prove UMC partnership economics or Fab 12i customer attach-rate.
next_validation_step: Identify potential UMC-relevant partner models and compare cost, cycle time, yield, and customer pull.
decision_implication: Supports considering a partner-first packaging / OSAT workstream, subject to UMC internal validation.

location: Yen Phong II-C Industrial Park, Bac Ninh
activity: Advanced system-in-package assembly and test manufacturing campus.
investment_amount: Source identifies major Vietnam campus investment; refreshed amount needed before Stage 1.
capacity_workforce_indicator: Capacity and workforce detail require source-level validation.
why_it_matters_for_umc: Indicates Vietnam has back-end and electronics manufacturing gravity that may create customer-engineering adjacency.
what_it_supports: Vietnam as customer engineering / EMS watchlist.
what_it_does_not_prove: It does not prove UMC customer overlap, local FAE workload, or revenue mechanism.
next_validation_step: Map Fab 12i customers with Vietnam EMS or assembly flows and identify support workflows.
decision_implication: Supports considering a partner-first packaging / OSAT workstream, subject to UMC internal validation.

company_organization: Thailand Eastern Economic Corridor Electronics / Automotive Cluster
activity: Industrial policy and manufacturing cluster for automotive, electronics, and advanced industries.
scale_indicator: National priority industrial corridor with electronics and automotive relevance.
investment_amount: Project-specific semiconductor incentive amount is a data gap.
capacity_workforce_indicator: Semiconductor-specific engineering capacity by function is a data gap.
why_it_matters_for_umc: Could indicate mature-node, power, display, or industrial customer demand near Thai manufacturing clusters.
what_it_supports: Thailand as automotive / power specialty watchlist.
what_it_does_not_prove: It does not prove named UMC accounts, wafer demand, or specialty-process revenue.
next_validation_step: Validate named automotive, power, and industrial customers against UMC product families.
decision_implication: Supports commercial sensing and account-development workstream, subject to named customer validation.

scale_indicator: Longstanding multinational semiconductor operations in the Philippines.
investment_amount: Project-specific comparable investment amount is a data gap.
why_it_matters_for_umc: Supports the view that the Philippines has semiconductor operations and support-talent relevance.
what_it_supports: Philippines as limited support / ATP watchlist.
what_it_does_not_prove: It does not prove UMC-specific support workload, FAE availability, or escalation economics.
next_validation_step: Define support workflows and test whether local talent can handle Fab 12i customer escalations.
decision_implication: Supports technical support workflow investigation, subject to workload and talent validation.

activity: Electronics manufacturing, industrial park, and logistics adjacency to Singapore.
scale_indicator: Industrial and logistics cluster near Singapore.
investment_amount: UMC-relevant semiconductor investment amount is a data gap.
capacity_workforce_indicator: Semiconductor-specific engineering workforce is a data gap.
why_it_matters_for_umc: Could be monitored for long-term customer, logistics, or policy adjacency.
what_it_supports: Indonesia as long-term IC design / policy watchlist.
what_it_does_not_prove: It does not prove foundry readiness, UMC customer density, partner maturity, or utility readiness.
next_validation_step: Monitor named customer demand, partner maturity, and policy specificity before considering Stage 1.
decision_implication: Supports commercial sensing and account-development workstream, subject to named customer validation.
